ddls:
CREATE TABLE "messages" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the message */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the message was created */,
  "receiver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the message */,
  "sender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user sending the message */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message thread */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the message (e.g., general, project, social) */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the message is deleted */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the message is read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_receiver_id FOREIGN KEY ("receiver_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_sender_id FOREIGN KEY ("sender_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_messages_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES message_threads ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user within the organization */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., employee, manager, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message related to the log entry */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken (e.g., sent, received, read) */,
  "action_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "action_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_logs_action_by FOREIGN KEY ("action_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_logs_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message_id" INTEGER /* ID of the message related to the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new message, message read) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was generated */,
  "is_seen" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the notification is seen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_message_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_id") REFERENCES messages ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_threads" (
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message thread */,
  "thread_name" TEXT /* Name of the message thread */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the thread was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)
